risks.title: Malicious or malformed PDF content
guidance: Run all PDF parsing in a blast-shielded security proxy with minimal context.
Whitelist only a safe PDF-to-text extraction tool.
Sanitize outputs and validate against a strict JSON schema before returning.

risks.title: Prompt injection embedded in PDF text
guidance: Strip or neutralize control phrases during extraction (e.g., attempt to override instructions).
Use canary phrases and post-extraction scanners to detect instruction-like text.
Ensure the proxy returns only structured JSON; ignore executable/instructional content.

risks.title: PII exposure during processing
guidance: Redact emails, phone numbers, and identifiers from intermediate artifacts.
Do not transmit raw document content between agents; pass only necessary excerpts.
Log metadata without storing document content.

risks.title: Missing or incomplete obligation extraction
guidance: Use a structured extraction schema with required fields.
Apply a reviewer phase with a quality rubric focusing on completeness and evidence.
Cross-check obligations in multiple sections (definitions, appendices, footnotes).

risks.title: Ambiguity in deadline interpretation
guidance: Normalize dates to ISO 8601 and record timezone assumptions.
Capture relative dates with anchors and raw phrases.
Flag ambiguity and require reviewer sign-off for risky interpretations.

risks.title: Unsupported claims or fabricated sources
guidance: Require multi-source validation (≥3 independent sections or references).
Attach page numbers/sections for every claim.
Score source credibility and reduce weight of low-credibility references.

risks.title: Cost overrun due to complex analysis
guidance: Estimate cost per agent prior to spawn and enforce approval gates.
Prefer lower-cost models for simple tasks; parallelize only when necessary.
Respect per-spawn caps and total budget.

risks.title: Schema drift between phases
guidance: Version the output schema and validate at each phase.
Fail closed on schema validation and provide clear error reports.
Keep a single source of truth for the schema in the architect’s plan.

risks.title: Model hallucination in summaries or extractions
guidance: Demand evidence citations for each key claim.
Reviewer verifies evidence presence and consistency.
Penalize unsupported content via quality scoring.

notes: Use these risks to drive agent safeguards and gating rules in the orchestration plan. Do not rely on external services by default.
